title: Tagged Partition
statement: Let $a,b \in \mathbb{R}$ with $a < b$. A list $\dot{\mathcal{P}}=\{([x_{i-1},x_i],t_i)\}_{i=1}^n$ is a tagged partition of $[a,b]$ if and only if the subintervals determine a partition $P=\{[x_{i-1},x_i]\}_{i=1}^n$ of $[a,b]$ with $a=x_0 < x_1 < \cdots < x_n=b$, and each tag satisfies $t_i \in [x_{i-1},x_i]$ for $i=1,\ldots,n$. Equivalently, a tagged partition is exactly a partition $P$ of $[a,b]$ together with one tag chosen inside every subinterval of $P$.
